KB, MB, and GB show up constantly when you use a phone or computer, yet many people have only a vague sense of what they actually mean. This article puts the units in plain terms — with concrete figures for how large photos and videos really are.

Webページ閲覧は1ページ約1MB、音楽1曲は約5MB、動画視聴は1時間で約1G: A typical webpage takes about 1 MB, a song is around 5 MB, and streaming video uses roughly 1 GB per hour. When phone storage is tight, deleting videos first is always the quickest win.
計算の落とし穴：「1000倍」と「1024倍」の混乱とハードディスク容量の謎: The 1,000 vs. 1,024 Trap: Why Your Storage Seems Smaller Than Advertised
パソコンやスマートフォンのスペック表記で、「128GBのモデルを買ったのに、実際: Have you ever bought a "128 GB" phone or PC only to find that the usable capacity shown by the device is noticeably smaller? There's a historical reason for this discrepancy in how data is calculated.
データの基本単位であるバイト（Byte）において、数学的な単位系（十進法）では: In the standard decimal (base-10) system, 1 KB = 1,000 bytes and 1 MB = 1,000 KB. But computers run on binary (base-2), so internally they treat 1 KB as 2¹⁰ = 1,024 bytes, and 1 MB as 1,024 KB.
スマートフォンやハードディスクのメーカーは一般的に「十進法（1GB＝1,000,: Smartphone and hard drive manufacturers advertise capacity using the decimal definition (1 GB = 1,000,000,000 bytes), while operating systems like Windows display capacity using the binary definition (1 GB = 1,073,741,824 bytes). This discrepancy makes the physical storage appear about 7.4% smaller on your computer. It is not a defect — your device is perfectly fine.
